objective: Test whether the Wave 62 all-left capacity-clearing source improves dynamic unseen-value/signature pre-materialization support over the Wave 65 outer-left source.
non_negotiables: This is a pre-materialization diagnostic and must not be treated as selected exact support.
Rows must still require materialization, replay, leakage, and baseline gates before promotion.
The report must compare against Wave 63, Wave 64, and Wave 65 selector results.
A partial unlock must record the remaining support gap explicitly.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a dynamic unseen-value/signature pairing preflight for the Wave 62 all-left supply source.
outputs: all-left dynamic pairing preflight CLI command
acceptance: The report records all-left pre-materialization selected pairs and topology counts.
The report records rejection counts before materialization.
Docs state that the report is not selected exact support.
Docs state the remaining gap to the 150-row rpf50 target.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 66 as an all-left dynamic-pairing partial unlock that improves Wave 65 but remains short of rpf50 and pre-materialization only.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that all-left dynamic preflight reaches 145 pairs.
Docs state that rpf50 still needs 150 rows and remains 5 rows short before materialization.
Docs compare against Wave 63's 12 rows, Wave 64's 4 rows, and Wave 65's 137 rows.
Docs route next work toward closing the last five preflight rows or materialization-aware dynamic selection.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_66_all_left_dynamic_pairing_preflight.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 66 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 66 all-left dynamic pairing preflight report is valid JSON.
